overview.summary: The ING module handles ingestion of reference data and daily updates from multiple external source types, generates derived sources, normalizes data against the product canonical, and exposes query/export APIs for downstream consumption. It is the operational core of NMD — everything between source and consumer.
overview.purpose: This document specifies the functional requirements for the Multi-Source Ingestion, Normalization & Query module within the Narwhal Market Data platform. It translates PC-NMD-0001 requirements FR-002 through FR-008 and FR-010 into detailed, testable specifications.
overview.audience: Developers, QA engineers, data owners, and downstream system integrators.

in_scope: Source onboarding — configuring and registering new data sources via common framework (FR-010)
Reference data ingestion — product static, contracts, with SCD Type 2 persistence (FR-002)
Daily update ingestion — settlement prices, margin rates, corporate actions per business date (FR-003)
Multi-source type support — exchange websites, public APIs, SFTP, structured documents, data feeds (FR-004)
Derived source processing — transform and reshape already-ingested data for customized export formats (FR-005)
Data normalization, mapping control, and validation linked to product canonical (FR-006)
Centralized ingestion framework — scheduling, run tracking, snapshot persistence (FR-007)
Reprocessing — authorized re-ingestion with delta versioning for corrections
Settlement history — versioned daily updates with business date tagging
Query and export APIs for downstream system consumption (FR-008)
out_of_scope: UI for data owners to manage sources, ingestion, and data (FR-009)
Real-time streaming and subscription-based data delivery (Phase 3)
Direct integration or data delivery to downstream systems (Phase 2)

items.definition: A point-in-time extract of data from one or more sources, used as input to the normalization pipeline.

A source that transforms and reshapes already-ingested data from one or more upstream sources into customized export formats, rather than pulling from an external system. Constructed by parsing source data in a configured step sequence where later steps override earlier values.
The centralized component that manages, schedules, tracks, and monitors the processing of all data sources.
A pluggable component that handles connectivity and parsing for a specific source type (e.g., SFTP, REST API, website scraper).

Slowly Changing Dimension Type 2 — a persistence pattern that tracks changes over time using effective_date / expiry_date ranges. A new record version is created when content changes; the previous version is expired, not overwritten.
Authorized re-ingestion of a source for a given business date, triggered when corrections or restatements are received. Increments the version and persists only delta records.
A record persisted during reprocessing that contains only the changed values compared to the previous version for the same business date.
The trading or valuation date to which a daily update record belongs, as opposed to the calendar date when the data was ingested.
The combination of source, entity, and record_type used to identify a unique record for SCD Type 2 change detection in reference data.
Storage tier containing current, non-expired SCD Type 2 reference data records. This is the data served by the query API.
Storage tier containing daily update records from the last 7 days, latest version per business date. This is the data served by the query API.
Separate database containing expired SCD Type 2 reference data records. Retained for audit and historical queries.
Separate database containing superseded daily update versions and daily update records older than 7 days before the current day snapshot. Retained for audit and historical queries.

business_context.content: This module delivers on the core NMD value proposition — eliminating direct vendor/exchange dependencies by centralizing ingestion and normalization. It provides a single, authoritative pipeline from external data sources to normalized, queryable data for downstream consumption.

items.name: Ingestion Framework
items.description: Orchestrates scheduled and on-demand ingestion runs

items.name: External Data Source
items.description: Exchanges, vendors — provides raw data via SFTP/API/website/files

items.name: Product Canonical
items.description: Provides master instrument records for normalization linking (FRD-NMD-0001)

items.name: Downstream System
items.description: Consumes normalized data via query/export APIs

items.name: Data Owner
items.description: Monitors ingestion, triggers manual runs, validates output

functional_overview.content:
It automates collection, validation, and normalization of reference data and daily updates to the canonical entity. It also supports derived sources that transform and reshape already-ingested data for customized export formats.

The module processes two distinct data categories that share the same source adapter framework, scheduling, and validation approach, but differ in persistence and retention behavior:

Reference Data — Static or semi-static product attributes such as contract specifications, multipliers, and payment methods. Reference data is persisted using SCD Type 2 with date ranges (effective_date / expiry_date). A new snapshot is created only when content changes for the same composite key (source, entity, record_type). Expired records are retained in another database for reference.

Daily Updates — Settlement prices, margin rates, and corporate actions received per business date. Daily update records are tagged to a specific business date with a version starting at 1. When corrections arrive via authorized reprocessing, the version increments and only delta records (changed values) are persisted in the new version. Older versions are retained in another database for reference. Records of dates a week before the current day snapshot will also be moved to that database.

The module covers: source onboarding, scheduling, run tracking, normalization, mapping control, snapshot persistence, validation, daily update ingestion and validation, settlement history, reprocessing, derived source processing, and query.

items.initiates: FR-ING-007, FR-ING-008, FR-ING-010, FR-ING-011, FR-ING-012 — pull from configured sources

items.trigger: Manual ingestion trigger by data owner
items.initiates: FR-ING-007, FR-ING-008, FR-ING-010, FR-ING-011, FR-ING-012 — on-demand pull for specific source

items.trigger: Source file/data available (e.g. SFTP file landed)
items.initiates: FR-ING-007, FR-ING-008, FR-ING-010, FR-ING-011, FR-ING-012 — event-driven ingestion
FR-ING-014, FR-ING-015, FR-ING-016 — triggers normalization and snapshot derivation
FR-ING-024 through FR-ING-028 — API serves normalized data

items.trigger: Authorized reprocessing request for a source and business date
items.initiates: FR-ING-032 — re-ingest with version increment, delta persistence

items.trigger: Daily retention window shift (current day advances)
items.initiates: FR-ING-039 — move daily update records older than 7 days to archive DB

items.trigger: SCD Type 2 expiry detected during reference data ingestion
items.initiates: FR-ING-037 — move expired reference records to archive DB

requirements.description: System shall support ingestion from exchange websites via HTTP/HTTPS
requirements.acceptance_criteria: At least one exchange website source adapter successfully ingests data

requirements.description: System shall support ingestion from public APIs (REST/JSON)
requirements.acceptance_criteria: At least one REST API source adapter successfully ingests data

requirements.description: System shall support ingestion from SFTP file drops
requirements.acceptance_criteria: SFTP adapter connects, detects new files, and ingests successfully

requirements.description: System shall support ingestion from structured documents (CSV, XML, PDF)
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Document adapter parses CSV, XML, and PDF formats and extracts data correctly

requirements.description: System shall support ingestion from data feeds
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Feed adapter connects and ingests data from at least one feed source

requirements.description: Each source type shall be handled by a pluggable source adapter with a common interface
requirements.acceptance_criteria: New source adapter can be added without modifying core ingestion framework code

requirements.description: System shall support onboarding of new data sources via a common framework without changes to core ingestion logic
requirements.acceptance_criteria: New source onboarded and producing data without code changes to core framework

requirements.description: System shall provide a source configuration interface to define connection details, schedule, mapping rules, and validation rules per source
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Source configuration created with all required fields; source activates on schedule

requirements.description: System shall validate source configuration before activating a new source for scheduled ingestion
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Invalid configuration rejected with clear error; source not activated until valid

requirements.description: System shall ingest product static data (contract specs, tick sizes, lot sizes) from configured sources
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Product static data ingested and stored as versioned source snapshot

requirements.description: System shall ingest contract listings (expiry dates, series, strike prices) from configured sources
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Contract listings ingested and stored as versioned source snapshot

requirements.description: System shall persist ingested reference data using SCD Type 2 with effective_date / expiry_date ranges. A new snapshot is created only when content changes for the same composite key (source, entity, record_type)
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Unchanged records do not generate new versions; changed records create new SCD Type 2 entry with correct date ranges

requirements.description: System shall ingest daily settlement prices from configured sources
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Settlement prices ingested with correct business date and version tagging

requirements.description: System shall ingest margin rates from configured sources
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Margin rates ingested and stored as dated source snapshot

requirements.description: System shall ingest corporate events from configured sources
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Corporate events ingested and stored as dated source snapshot

requirements.description: System shall tag daily update records to a specific business date with a version starting at 1. When corrections arrive via authorized reprocessing, the version increments and only delta records (changed values) are persisted in the new version
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Version increments on reprocessing; only changed values persisted in new version; previous versions retained in archive

requirements.description: System shall support authorized reprocessing of a source for a given business date, incrementing the version and persisting only delta records
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Reprocessing creates new version with only delta records; requires authorization

requirements.description: System shall maintain settlement history — all versions of daily updates per business date queryable for audit
requirements.acceptance_criteria: All versions of a daily update for a given business date retrievable via API or archive query

requirements.description: System shall support derived sources that transform and reshape already-ingested data from upstream sources into customized export formats, constructed by parsing source data in a configured step sequence where later steps override earlier values
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Derived source produces correct output from upstream ingested data in configured step order

requirements.description: System shall normalize ingested data to a standard internal format
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Output data conforms to internal schema regardless of source format

requirements.description: System shall link each normalized record to its corresponding product canonical record
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Every normalized record carries a canonical record ID or is flagged as unmatched

requirements.description: System shall flag records that cannot be matched to a canonical record for manual review
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Unmatched records visible with unmatched status; data owner can review and resolve

requirements.description: System shall provide mapping control to define field-level transformation and normalization rules per source
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Mapping rules applied during normalization; field transformations produce expected output

requirements.description: System shall validate ingested data against configured validation rules before persistence
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Records failing validation are flagged and not persisted to active layer

requirements.description: System shall provide a centralized registry of all configured data sources
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Source registry lists all configured sources with connection details and schedules

requirements.description: System shall support scheduled ingestion runs (configurable per source)
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Scheduled runs execute at configured times without manual intervention

requirements.description: System shall support manual on-demand ingestion for a specific source
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Data owner can trigger ingestion for a specific source and monitor its progress

requirements.description: System shall detect source file availability and trigger ingestion automatically
requirements.acceptance_criteria: File landing on SFTP or equivalent triggers ingestion within configured detection interval

requirements.description: System shall track ingestion run status (pending, running, completed, failed) per source
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Run status queryable in real time; historical runs retained

requirements.description: System shall log all ingestion activity with source, timestamp, record counts, and outcome
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Audit log contains source ID, run timestamp, records processed, records failed, and final outcome

requirements.description: System shall expose a query API to retrieve normalized reference data by instrument, product type, or exchange
requirements.acceptance_criteria: API returns correct reference data filtered by requested parameters

requirements.description: System shall expose a query API to retrieve daily settlement prices by instrument and date/date range
requirements.acceptance_criteria: API returns correct settlement prices for requested instrument and date range

requirements.description: System shall expose a query API to retrieve margin rates by instrument and date
requirements.acceptance_criteria: API returns correct margin rates for requested instrument and date

requirements.description: System shall support bulk export of normalized data as file download (CSV/JSON)
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Bulk export generates downloadable file in requested format with correct data

requirements.description: System shall support filtering, pagination, and sorting on all query endpoints
requirements.acceptance_criteria: All query endpoints accept filter, page, page_size, and sort parameters and return correct results

requirements.description: System shall support query of archived data (expired reference records, older daily update versions and dates) from the archive database
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Archive query returns correct historical data from the archive database

requirements.description: System shall move expired SCD Type 2 reference data records to the archive database
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Expired reference records no longer in active DB; present in archive DB

requirements.description: System shall move superseded daily update versions to the archive database
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Only latest version per business date remains in active DB; older versions in archive DB

requirements.description: System shall move daily update records older than 7 days before the current day snapshot to the archive database
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Active DB contains only last 7 days of daily updates; older records in archive DB

items.rule: A source snapshot must be fully ingested before normalization begins — no partial processing

Derived sources transform already-ingested data by parsing in a configured step sequence — each step overlays the previous, so later steps override earlier values for the same field. Derived sources use the same adapter framework as external sources.

Daily update records are tagged to a business date with version starting at 1. Corrections via authorized reprocessing increment the version; only delta records (changed values) are persisted in the new version. Older versions are retained in the archive database.
Normalization must not create or modify product canonical records — only link to existing ones
Ingestion runs for the same source must not execute concurrently — queue or reject if already running
All API query results return data from the latest completed normalization run only — in-progress data is never exposed

Reference data changes are detected by comparing incoming data against the current active record for the same composite key (source, entity, record_type). A new SCD Type 2 record is created only when content differs — unchanged records do not generate new versions.
Reprocessing requires explicit authorization — the system shall not allow ad-hoc re-ingestion without a controlled trigger.
Daily update records older than 7 days before the current day snapshot are moved to the archive database. This retention window applies per business date, not per ingestion date.

items.system_behavior: Retry up to configured max attempts with backoff; log each attempt
items.user_visible_outcome: Ingestion run marked 'Failed' with error detail; alert raised

items.system_behavior: Abort ingestion for that source; do not process partial data
items.user_visible_outcome: Run marked 'Failed — Schema Mismatch'; data owner notified

items.trigger_condition: Record cannot be matched to a canonical record during normalization
items.system_behavior: Record flagged as 'Unmatched'; normalization continues for remaining records
items.user_visible_outcome: Unmatched records visible in query API with unmatched status; data owner review required

items.trigger_condition: Duplicate ingestion run triggered (same source, same schedule window)
items.user_visible_outcome: Data owner informed run was skipped — previous run still active

items.system_behavior: Log warning; do not overwrite previous snapshot with empty data
items.user_visible_outcome: Run marked 'Completed — Empty'; previous snapshot remains current

items.system_behavior: Reject the request; do not re-ingest
items.user_visible_outcome: Request denied with 'Unauthorized reprocessing' message; event logged

items.trigger_condition: Reprocessing produces zero delta records (no changes detected)
items.system_behavior: Log info; no new version created
items.user_visible_outcome: Run marked 'Completed — No Deltas'; current version unchanged

items.trigger_condition: Archive DB unavailable when retention move triggered
items.system_behavior: Retain records in active DB; retry archive move on next cycle
items.user_visible_outcome: Alert raised; records remain queryable in active DB until archive succeeds

items.trigger_condition: SCD Type 2 change detection fails (composite key mismatch or corrupt data)
items.system_behavior: Abort ingestion for that source; flag records for review
items.user_visible_outcome: Run marked 'Failed — Key Resolution Error'; data owner notified

apis_provided.name: Query Reference Data
apis_provided.description: Retrieve normalized reference data by instrument, product type, or exchange

apis_provided.name: Query Settlement Prices
apis_provided.description: Retrieve settlement prices by instrument and date/date range

apis_provided.name: Query Margin Rates
apis_provided.description: Retrieve margin rates by instrument and date

apis_provided.name: Ingestion Status
apis_provided.description: Query ingestion run status per source

apis_provided.name: Archive Query
apis_provided.description: Retrieve expired reference records, older daily update versions and dates from archive database

data_owned.notes: SCD Type 2 records; active in main DB, expired in archive DB
Business-date-tagged with versioning; last 7 days active, older in archive DB
Linked to canonical, serves query API

items.purpose: Linking normalized data to master records (FR-ING-016)

items.what_consumed: Raw market data (SFTP, API, website, files, feeds)

entities.name: Reference Data Record
entities.description: SCD Type 2 records with effective_date / expiry_date. Active records in main DB, expired records moved to archive DB

entities.name: Daily Update Record
entities.description: Business-date-tagged records with version counter. Latest 7 days in active DB, older records and superseded versions moved to archive DB

entities.name: Derived Source Output
entities.description: Output of derived source processing, reshaping ingested data for export formats

entities.name: Normalized Record
entities.description: Linked to canonical, serves query API

entities.name: Ingestion Run Log
entities.description: Operational audit trail for all ingestion activity

entities.name: Source Configuration
entities.description: Source registry, connection details, schedules, mapping rules, validation rules, step sequences

entities.name: Unmatched Record
entities.description: Flagged for data owner review during normalization

data_requirements.data_retention:
Two-tier storage with separate retention behavior per data category:

Reference Data:
  Active DB — current, non-expired SCD Type 2 records (latest effective version per composite key).
  Archive DB — expired SCD Type 2 records, retained for audit and historical queries.

Daily Updates:
  Active DB — records from the last 7 days (latest version per business date).
  Archive DB — superseded versions and records older than 7 days before current day snapshot.

Both archive databases are queryable via FR-ING-036.

items.description: Daily settlement file processing must complete within SLA window (ref NFR-001: T+1 by 08:00)
items.design_impact: Pipeline must be optimized for batch throughput; may require parallel source processing

items.description: Query API responses must meet latency target (ref NFR-004: <500ms p95)
items.design_impact: Normalized data must be pre-indexed/materialized, not computed on query

items.description: All ingested data must be traceable to its source with full audit log (ref NFR-003)
items.design_impact: Every record carries source ID, snapshot version, ingestion run ID

items.description: Ingestion failures must trigger operational alerts (ref NFR-008)
items.design_impact: Framework must integrate with alerting; EXC-001 through EXC-009 all produce alerts

items.description: Source adapter addition must not require changes to core ingestion framework (ref NFR-006)
items.design_impact: Adapter interface must be stable and pluggable

matrix.description: Ingest reference data from external sources
Ingest daily updates from external sources
Generate derived sources from ingested data
Support onboarding new data feeds via common framework

items.criteria: At least one source adapter successfully ingests reference data and daily updates from an external source
Derived source processing produces correct output from upstream ingested data in configured step order
All normalized records are linked to a canonical record or flagged as unmatched
Query API returns normalized data with <500ms p95 latency
Ingestion framework tracks run status and logs all activity with full source traceability
Settlement price versioning works correctly — multiple intraday updates tracked via delta versioning, latest tagged
All nine exception scenarios (EXC-001 through EXC-009) behave as specified
SCD Type 2 persistence works correctly — new versions created only on content change, expired records moved to archive
Reprocessing creates new version with delta records only when authorized
Data retention operates correctly — daily updates older than 7 days moved to archive DB
New source can be onboarded via configuration without changes to core ingestion logic

assumptions.description: Exchange files for Futures and Options and FX are accessible via public or permissioned sources (SFTP, website, API)
Exchange selection for Futures and Options will be confirmed before detailed design begins

constraints.description: Data types limited to Futures and Options and FX in Phase 1
Exchange selection for Futures and Options TBC

items.description: Exchange or official source access credentials or agreements for Futures and Options (TBC)
Confirmation of target exchanges for Phase 1 Futures and Options data
Product Canonical module (FRD-NMD-0001) operational for normalization linking

entries.author: Leo
entries.description: Major update: revised functional overview with two-category data model (reference data with SCD Type 2, daily updates with delta versioning). Added source onboarding (FR-010), reprocessing, derived source processing, mapping control, validation, data retention rules, and archive query. Total requirements increased from 28 to 39. Added 4 new exception scenarios, 3 new business rules, 3 new event triggers.
